sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# I'm at position 43 (col 43). Need to go to position 22 (col 22).
# That's 3 LEFT moves.
# Then I need to cycle UP/DOWN to reach fb80c9b281b063da at position 22.

# From my earlier scan, position 22's cycle was:
# in_top=[False, False, False, False, False, False, True]
# The True position is index 6 (last one), which is fb80c9b281b063da
# The cycle started from c66a83c4e7c00e20 (index 0)

# Current state at pos 22 is c66a83c4e7c00e20 (index 0)
# To reach fb80c9b281b063da (index 6), I need 6 UPs (or 1 DOWN since it cycles)

# Let me go LEFT 3 times to reach pos 22, then UP 6 times (or DOWN 1)
predict(expect_change=True, note="Go LEFT 3 to pos 22, then DOWN 1 to reach fb80c9b281b063da")
action(['LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT', 'DOWN'])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
current_pink = {}
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'B':
        br = n['boundary']
        if br:
            min_r = min(p[0] for p in br)
            min_c = min(p[1] for p in br)
            if min_r == 52:
                current_pink[min_c] = n['hash']

print("Pink after change:", current_pink)
print("Pos 22 hash:", current_pink.get(22))

# Check if level completed
print("Done:", last_action_result.get('done', False))
print("Level completed:", last_action_result.get('level_completed', False))
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
